title: The Problem With Popplers

jokes.text: I hate the Moochers. They offer you a drink... but their wallet's always in the other pants... which they borrowed from me!
jokes.setup: Standard complaint about freeloading friends who never pay
jokes.punchline: The escalation that they borrowed the pants with the wallet from the complainer himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with perfect reveal timing. The borrowed pants twist is genuinely surprising and clever.

jokes.text: Fight to the death. I'll cook the loser. Work his gut. I like it tender.
jokes.setup: No food situation creates tension
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual cannibalism suggestion with culinary preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only Bender would suggest this so casually. The 'work his gut' detail adds personality.

jokes.text: No, we're a billion miles from nowhere. It probably only has a Howard Johnson's.
jokes.setup: Remote planet location creates expectation of no amenities
jokes.punchline: Howard Johnson's as the worst-case dining scenario
jokes.explanation: Solid but predictable brand-name humor. Gets the job done but lacks originality.

jokes.text: I'm good at foraging. I found mushrooms on my bathmat.
jokes.setup: Claims foraging expertise to help with food situation
jokes.punchline: The 'expertise' is just finding bathroom fungi
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment - naive confidence about disgusting personal hygiene habits.

jokes.text: I found some rocks! You eat rocks, right? Not even sautéed in a little mud?
jokes.setup: Fry desperately trying to find food for Bender
jokes.punchline: The hopeful follow-up about mud preparation shows his cluelessness
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Fry's earnest but misguided helpfulness.

jokes.text: Look! A ditch full of fried shrimp! Are you blind? Looks more like a hole full of fried prawns.
jokes.setup: Fry excitedly identifies food
jokes.punchline: Bender's 'correction' is essentially the same thing with fancier words
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that captures Bender's pretentious personality. The semantic similarity makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: They're great! They're like sex! Except I'm having them!
jokes.setup: Fry describing how good the popplers taste
jokes.punchline: The self-deprecating admission about his sex life
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character integration. The unexpected self-burn makes a simple comparison memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: They're tasty. What about Tasty-cles? Not that. Why not? It sounds like frozen mountain oysters on a stick. You know, Test-cicles.
jokes.setup: Naming the new food product
jokes.punchline: Amy's oblivious sexual innuendo that she doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual pun humor. The character work helps but it's fairly predictable innuendo.

jokes.text: Slow down there, Sigmund! I can't stand poor people getting free food.
jokes.setup: Someone eating free samples enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: The vendor's specific hatred of poor people getting anything free
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven dark humor about capitalism. The 'Sigmund' adds personality to the insult.

jokes.text: Where do you want those rat droppings you ordered?
jokes.setup: Normal food service delivery context
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of rat droppings as a restaurant ingredient
jokes.explanation: Straightforward gross-out humor about restaurant hygiene. Functional but unoriginal.

jokes.text: You're a guy who eats at Fishy Joe's? Hell, no! I am Fishy Joe!
jokes.setup: Assumption about customer identity
jokes.punchline: The emphatic rejection followed by proud ownership reveal
jokes.explanation: Standard reveal joke with good delivery energy but predictable structure.

jokes.text: I'll be rich! You too, but that doesn't excite me.
jokes.setup: Celebrating getting rich from the deal
jokes.punchline: The afterthought dismissal of others' success
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's self-centered enthusiasm. Well-structured punchline.

jokes.text: Pop a Poppler at Fishy Joe's... Pick them, lick them, chew them, stick them, if you won't sue. Shove one up your nose
jokes.setup: Standard commercial jingle format
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd and dangerous consumption methods
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with good parody of commercial jingles. The legal disclaimer and nose-shoving are genuinely absurd.

jokes.text: Unless this is a nude love-in, get the hell off my property!
jokes.setup: Confronting protesters on his property
jokes.punchline: The specific exception for nude love-ins
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Joe's priorities. Well-constructed exception joke.

jokes.text: You can't own property, man! I can, because I'm not a penniless hippie!
jokes.setup: Hippie philosophy about property ownership
jokes.punchline: Joe's matter-of-fact rebuttal based on financial status
jokes.explanation: Solid character conflict humor. The directness of the rebuttal is effective.

jokes.text: No, it isn't. We taught a lion to eat tofu.
jokes.setup: Argument about natural animal behavior
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim about converting a lion to vegetarianism
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor that perfectly captures hippie activist mentality. The impossibility makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Okay, we won't eat you! I'll go get some more bricks.
jokes.setup: Discussion about not eating things that feel pain
jokes.punchline: The casual threat escalation to violence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from verbal to physical threat. Character-appropriate response.

jokes.text: Mama!
jokes.setup: Leela examining what appears to be just food
jokes.punchline: The shocking revelation that it can speak
jokes.explanation: Perfect story turning point with maximum economy. Single word completely changes the episode's direction.

jokes.text: Congratulations! I assume Amy is the father?
jokes.setup: Leela announces the Poppler called her mama
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's confused gender assumptions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - his senile confusion creates absurd biological assumptions.

jokes.text: She's hallucinating from lack of Popplers. Eat some now!
jokes.setup: Trying to explain Leela's claim about talking Popplers
jokes.punchline: Prescribing more of the supposedly addictive substance
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment - his solution to withdrawal is more of the substance.

jokes.text: Parrots talk and we eat them, right? Maybe it learned as a parlor trick. Like Fry. Like Fry! Like Fry!
jokes.setup: Comparing talking animals to establish intelligence levels
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately demonstrating parrot-like repetition behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy setup where Fry immediately proves the point about mindless repetition. Perfect character integration and structure.

jokes.text: There's one way, and only one way, to determine if an animal is intelligent. Dissect its brain.
jokes.setup: Need to determine Poppler intelligence scientifically
jokes.punchline: The Professor's destructive testing method
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth dark science humor. The certainty about the destructive method is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: No! Mama! Stop Grandpa!
jokes.setup: Professor about to dissect the Poppler
jokes.punchline: Poppler calling him 'Grandpa' while begging for help
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the family dynamic joke. The 'Grandpa' recognition adds humor to the tension.

jokes.text: You, maybe.
jokes.setup: Leela claims Popplers are as intelligent as 'you or me'
jokes.punchline: Passerby's cutting response about Leela's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Classic comeback structure with perfect economy, but lacks originality or character specificity.

jokes.text: Stop eating them with honey mustard sauce, tangy sweet and sour sauce! Stop eating the fiesta Popplers salad! Stop taking advantage of the cheaper 12-pack!
jokes.setup: Leela trying to discourage Poppler consumption
jokes.punchline: Her detailed descriptions making them sound more appealing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic structure where the protest becomes advertising. Original concept with perfect execution showing unintended consequences.

jokes.text: Stop eating them with honey mustard sauce, tangy sweet and sour sauce! Stop eating the fiesta Popplers salad!
jokes.setup: Leela trying to protest eating popplers
jokes.punchline: Her protest accidentally becomes advertising copy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic structure - her anti-advertising becomes advertising. Great escalation pattern.

jokes.text: Taronga? That's her name, Philip.
jokes.setup: TV host introduces Leela with elaborate name
jokes.punchline: Casual correction about her actual name being Philip's confusion
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about TV show name confusion. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Ca-ca.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to get Poppler to say 'mama' on TV
jokes.punchline: Poppler says something inappropriate instead
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassing moment humor. The setup works but the payoff is predictable.

jokes.text: The reason we don't eat people is the lousy taste.
jokes.setup: Discussion about ethics of eating intelligent creatures
jokes.punchline: Reducing cannibalism taboo to mere taste preference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that subverts moral arguments with amoral practicality. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Ca-ca head. Mean, old ca-ca head.
jokes.setup: Poppler responding to Fishy Joe's arguments
jokes.punchline: Child-like insults during serious ethical debate
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains the Poppler's child-like personality during adult discussion.

jokes.text: Stop it! I call murder on that!
jokes.setup: Fishy Joe eating Poppler on live TV
jokes.punchline: Hippie's dramatic declaration using playground language
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific language that fits the hippie's earnest but childish approach to activism.

jokes.text: I'm willing to grant it's murder. The real issue is... Who's gonna stop me?
jokes.setup: Ethical argument about eating intelligent creatures
jokes.punchline: Joe's pragmatic nihilism about consequences
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that cuts through moral arguments with brutal pragmatism. Great character moment and highly quotable.

jokes.text: And crap like I've never seen!
jokes.setup: Professional news anchor reports technical difficulties
jokes.punchline: He breaks character to add crude commentary
jokes.explanation: Decent humor from professional facade breaking down, though the phrasing is a bit awkward.

jokes.text: Turn down the TV, Indunda.
jokes.setup: Dramatic alien transmission interrupting broadcast
jokes.punchline: Domestic request during invasion announcement
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of domestic mundane with alien invasion drama. Perfect character moment for Lrrr.

jokes.text: When you know you can't scratch, that's when you really have to. Oh, yeah! That feels a lot better... What? It's still on?
jokes.setup: Lrrr thinks transmission is over
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing personal moment broadcast to Earth
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy concept with relatable embarrassment. Shows Lrrr's vulnerability beneath the intimidation.

jokes.text: It'd be great with quack-a-mole. Stop eating our young! And it's guacamole!
jokes.setup: Nixon commenting during serious alien negotiation
jokes.punchline: Lrrr correcting pronunciation while staying angry
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Nixon's food obsession during crisis and Lrrr's pedantic nature.

jokes.text: It's 198 billion, sir. Very well. You will provide us with 198 billion humans. And small fries. Lrrr! All right. Cottage cheese.
jokes.setup: Serious negotiation about human lives
jokes.punchline: Casual food ordering during genocide discussion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy mixing casual dining with mass extinction. Perfect character work showing misplaced priorities.

jokes.text: I'll do my part. Kif? Clear my schedule.
jokes.setup: Need for super-virile men to repopulate Earth quickly
jokes.punchline: Zapp immediately volunteering with executive efficiency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - his ego and libido immediately see this as an opportunity for glory.

jokes.text: Just wipe out humanity. Get it over with. It's the waiting I can't stand.
jokes.setup: Discussion about humanity's fate
jokes.punchline: Bender's impatience about extinction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - reduces human extinction to personal inconvenience. Dark but brilliant.

jokes.text: I filled up on nuts negotiating.
jokes.setup: Announcing the negotiation results
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning snacking during humanity's crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nixon's casual approach to major crises.

jokes.text: Try our new Extreme Walrus Juice! One hundred percent fresh-squeezed walrus! Ride the walrus!
jokes.setup: Commercial during execution broadcast
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd walrus juice marketing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist commercial parody. 'Ride the walrus' is genuinely surreal and memorable.

jokes.text: You're just a carbon-based life form... but I'll always think of you as a big pile of titanium. Just like titanium.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to give Leela a heartfelt goodbye
jokes.punchline: His robot-centric compliment system and Fry's translation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment showing his inability to express human emotions except through robot metaphors. Touching and funny.

jokes.text: Someone's acting awfully aluminum.
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about being the one to face execution
jokes.punchline: Bender's metal-based insult for acting cheap/petty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback extending Bender's metal-based value system into insults. Original and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Tell it to Wingus and Dingus.
jokes.setup: Zapp presenting his rescue plan
jokes.punchline: Fry's dismissive made-up names
jokes.explanation: Good Fry-style nonsense dismissal. The made-up names have a nice rhythm and feel authentically stupid.

jokes.text: It doesn't look anything like me! The hair's all wrong.
jokes.setup: Zapp claims the orangutan looks exactly like Leela
jokes.punchline: Leela's vain concern about the wrong hair detail
jokes.explanation: Good character vanity joke. Leela focusing on hair when facing death by substitution is perfectly shallow.

jokes.text: It doesn't look anything like me! The hair's all wrong.
jokes.setup: Zapp presents an orangutan as Leela's double
jokes.punchline: Leela's main objection is the hair styling
jokes.explanation: Good character humor where Leela focuses on vanity concerns despite the obviously terrible disguise.

jokes.text: Please, I'm creating!
jokes.setup: Zapp calling Kif an expert stylist
jokes.punchline: Kif's artistic pretensions about ape grooming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif character moment - taking artistic pride in degrading tasks while maintaining dignity.

jokes.text: That's Leela! I'm seeing double!
jokes.setup: The orangutan has been styled to look like Leela
jokes.punchline: Characters' amazed reactions to the obvious deception
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag setup. The characters' amazement sells the joke but it's fairly predictable.

jokes.text: That comes with salad or soup. Salad. Ranch or vinaigrette? Balsamic or raspberry?
jokes.setup: Alien about to eat human in dramatic execution
jokes.punchline: Elaborate restaurant service during cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the mundane service industry into horrific context. The detail specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Would you like some human with your salt?
jokes.setup: Lrrr heavily salting his food
jokes.punchline: Waiter's reversal of the usual condiment question
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and structure. The reversal of condiment/main dish is clever and memorable.

jokes.text: Why'd you open your bong hole, you smelly hippie?
jokes.setup: Free Waterfall Jr. ruins the deception
jokes.punchline: Joe's drug-reference insult for speaking up
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult. 'Bong hole' is creative and fits Joe's crude businessman persona.

jokes.text: You'd sacrifice a beautiful woman for an attractive monkey? You must have smoked some bad granola.
jokes.setup: Criticizing the switch plan
jokes.punchline: Hippie's backwards priorities and granola drug reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect hippie character moment - the monkey gets 'attractive' while the woman is just 'beautiful,' plus granola as drug reference.

jokes.text: Go consummate yourself.
jokes.setup: Zapp mentions consummating their relationship
jokes.punchline: Leela's clever 'go f*** yourself' substitute
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay creating a new insult. Perfect economy and highly quotable. Creative use of 'consummate.'

jokes.text: Little one, get out of there! I'm going to count to Blorks!
jokes.setup: Jurr trying to save Leela from his father
jokes.punchline: Alien counting system threat
jokes.explanation: Good alien culture worldbuilding humor. 'Blorks' as a counting threat is appropriately alien.

jokes.text: Hear me out! There are many good reasons to eat! Hunger, boredom, wanting to be the world's fattest man!
jokes.setup: Fry tries to argue against revenge eating
jokes.punchline: His list of eating reasons escalates to absurd motivations
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from reasonable to absurd eating motivations. Perfect Fry logic where he misses the point spectacularly.

jokes.text: I think there was something funny in that hippie.
jokes.setup: Lrrr has just eaten the hippie and is acting strange
jokes.punchline: He suggests the hippie tasted 'funny' (implying drugs)
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor that explains Lrrr's sudden personality change. The implication that hippies are naturally drugged is perfect.

jokes.text: Dude, my hands are huge! They can touch anything but themselves. Oh, wait...
jokes.setup: Hippie experiencing drug effects from being eaten
jokes.punchline: Stoned realization about hand limitations, then immediate contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect stoner philosophy humor with immediate self-contradiction. Great character work and genuinely funny drug logic.

jokes.text: No, this one blew his money on lottery tickets.
jokes.setup: Leela pointing out dolphins are intelligent
jokes.punchline: This particular dolphin's poor financial decisions justify eating it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about selective ethics based on individual behavior rather than species intelligence. Original and quotable.

jokes.text: Pass the blowhole. Fluke, please. Quit hogging the bottle-nose! Toss me the speech center of the brain!
jokes.setup: Group eating dolphin at dinner
jokes.punchline: Escalating anatomically specific requests culminating in the brain speech center
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure building to the speech center punchline. Dark, anatomically specific, and perfectly timed escalation.

tmdb_overview: The crew discovers an irresistible source of food on a distant planet, and brings it back to Earth to be sold at the Fishy Joe's restaurant chain. But when it's discovered that the so-called "Popplers" are actually Omicronian babies, the Omicronians demand recompense.
